title: ferray-fft: complex FFTs require Complex<f64> input — no auto-promotion from real arrays
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's np.fft.fft() accepts real arrays and automatically promotes them to complex: np.fft.fft([1.0, 2.0, 3.0]) works directly. ferray-fft's fft() requires Array<Complex<f64>, D> — passing a real array is a type error. Users must manually convert: Array<f64> -> Vec<Complex<f64>> -> Array<Complex<f64>>. rfft() exists for real inputs but produces the half-spectrum. For full-spectrum FFT of real data, there should be either: (1) a convenience fft_real() function, (2) auto-promotion in fft() via a trait bound, or (3) From<Array<f64,D>> for Array<Complex<f64>,D> conversion.
